content.content: User has answered your questions: "WPF에서 체결 명령을 어떻게 보낼 건가요? 작업판 그림에서 홀 클릭 → 브릿지 서버가 좌표 전송? 아니면 TMflow가 브릿지 서버에 접속해서 좌표를 요청하는 방식?"="시나리오 작성 적용 - 하면 시나리오대로 체결-풀림 작업 할거임 ( 시나리오 작성 메뉴도 필요할듯)" user notes: 시나리오 작성 적용 - 하면 시나리오대로 체결-풀림 작업 할거임 ( 시나리오 작성 메뉴도 필요할듯), "브릿지 서버의 Compute Box(XML-RPC) 통신과 TMflow 소켓 통신을 동시에 할 건가요?"="브릿지 확장", "3D 뷰어와 작업판 시각화 화면 배치는?"="좌: 3D뷰어 / 우: 작업판+로그", "3D 로봇 뷰어의 실시간 업데이트는 필요한가요? (TMSVR 5891 연결)"="실시간 필수". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.

questions.question: WPF에서 체결 명령을 어떻게 보낼 건가요? 작업판 그림에서 홀 클릭 → 브릿지 서버가 좌표 전송? 아니면 TMflow가 브릿지 서버에 접속해서 좌표를 요청하는 방식?

options.label: TMflow → 브릿지 요청
options.description: TMflow가 브릿지 서버에 TCP 접속해서 '다음 좌표 달라' 요청 → 서버가 row,col 응답 → TMflow가 이동+체결 → 결과(토크) 서버로 전송

options.label: WPF에서 홀 클릭 → 체결
options.description: 사용자가 작업판에서 홀 클릭 → WPF가 TMSCT(5890)로 로봇에 직접 좌표 전송 → 체결 → 토크 결과 수신

options.label: 전체 자동 (순차)
options.description: 시작 버튼 → 135홀 순차 자동 체결. TMflow가 루프 돌면서 브릿지에서 pickup/tighten 처리, 완료마다 WPF에 토크 결과 전달

questions.question: 브릿지 서버의 Compute Box(XML-RPC) 통신과 TMflow 소켓 통신을 동시에 할 건가요?

options.label: 기존 sd_bridge 그대로
options.description: TMflow → 브릿지(TCP 5001) → Compute Box(XML-RPC 41414). 기존 프로토콜 유지, WPF는 로그/결과만 표시

options.label: 브릿지 확장
options.description: 기존 sd_bridge 기능 + TMflow에 좌표 전달하는 새 명령어 추가 (예: next_hole → row,col 응답)

options.label: 좌: 3D뷰어 / 우: 작업판+로그
options.description: 왼쪽에 3D 로봇, 오른쪽에 작업판 이미지 + 하단 로그

options.label: 탭 분리
options.description: 탭1: 3D 로봇뷰어, 탭2: 작업판+체결결과, 공통: 하단 로그

questions.question: 3D 로봇 뷰어의 실시간 업데이트는 필요한가요? (TMSVR 5891 연결)

options.label: 실시간 필수
options.description: TM12Viewer처럼 TMSVR 5891 연결해서 관절각도 실시간 반영

options.label: 불필요
options.description: 3D 뷰어 없이 작업판 시각화 + 브릿지 서버만

answers.WPF에서 체결 명령을 어떻게 보낼 건가요? 작업판 그림에서 홀 클릭 →: 시나리오 작성 적용 - 하면 시나리오대로 체결-풀림 작업 할거임 ( 시나리오 작성 메뉴도 필요할듯)

WPF에서 체결 명령을 어떻게 보낼 건가요? 작업판 그림에서 홀 클릭 →.notes: 시나리오 작성 적용 - 하면 시나리오대로 체결-풀림 작업 할거임 ( 시나리오 작성 메뉴도 필요할듯)

message.content:
Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
   - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
   - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
7. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
8. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
9. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>
<analysis>
[Your thought process, ensuring all points are covered thoroughly and accurately]
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   [Detailed description]

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - [Concept 1]
   - [Concept 2]
   - [...]

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - [File Name 1]
      - [Summary of why this file is important]
      - [Summary of the changes made to this file, if any]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [File Name 2]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [...]

4. Errors and fixes:
    - [Detailed description of error 1]:
      - [How you fixed the error]
      - [User feedback on the error if any]
    - [...]

5. Problem Solving:
   [Description of solved problems and ongoing troubleshooting]

6. All user messages: 
    - [Detailed non tool use user message]
    - [...]

7. Pending Tasks:
   - [Task 1]
   - [Task 2]
   - [...]

8. Current Work:
   [Precise description of current work]

9. Optional Next Step:
   [Optional Next step to take]

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:
<example>
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing the conversation focus on typescript code changes and also remember the mistakes you made and how you fixed them.
</example>

<example>
# Summary instructions
When you are using compact - please focus on test output and code changes. Include file reads verbatim.
</example>


IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any tools. You MUST respond with ONLY the <summary>...</summary> block as your text output.

content.text:
<analysis>
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. **Initial Context**: User pointed to `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot` directory - a TM12 robot project with OnRobot Screwdriver integration.

2. **sd_bridge.py torque reading issue**: User showed logs where tighten commands returned very low torque (0.07, 0.11) when target was 0.4 Nm. The bridge server reads torque after `wait_until_done()` returns, but the issue was that `wait_until_done()` could catch a premature `busy=false` before the operation actually started.

3. **Fix 1 - wait_start parameter**: Added `wait_start=True` to `wait_until_done()` for pickup/tighten/loosen. This first waits for busy=true (operation started), then waits for busy=false (operation completed). Also added 3-point torque reading debug.

4. **shank command blocking issue**: The shank command was blocking due to the new 3-second busy=true wait. TMflow was sending multiple shank commands that piled up. Fixed by adding `wait_start` parameter (default False for shank).

5. **shank non-blocking**: Further simplified shank handler to not wait for completion at all - just send `sd_move_shank` and return OK immediately.

6. **Torque confirmed working**: Second tighten showed torque=0.42 (target 0.4) - correct. Cleaned up debug code, kept single torque read.

7. **TMscript PTP issues**: User wanted pallet movement script. Several issues discovered:
   - `PTP("CPP", target, -1, -1, 0, false)` - speed/accel -1 is invalid (silently skipped)
   - `PTP("CPP", "PalletPoint1", ...)` - string point name not supported (Error 00048202)
   - Correct: `PTP("CPP", float[], 50, 200, 100, false)`
   - Need `QueueTag(1)` + `WaitQueueTag(1)` for PTP completion wait

8. **3-point interpolation formula**: Initially used `P2 + u*(P1-P2) + v*(P3-P2)` with P2 as origin. User said P1 is origin. Changed to `P1 + u*(P2-P1) + v*(P3-P1)`. But this was wrong because P3-P1 is diagonal. Correct formula: `P1 + u*(P2-P1) + v*(P3-P2)` where P3-P2 is pure row direction.

9. **Safety alarm recovery**: WebClient showed "안전 작동" active. `sd_get_error_code` returned 0, but `sd_get_z_safety_activated` returned True. Fixed by adding z_safety check to status command, auto-recovery via `sd_move_shank(0, 0)`.

10. **New WPF project planning**: User wants to create a new WPF app at `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TmRobot Ex` combining:
    - 3D robot viewer (from TM12Viewer)
    - Bridge server (C# port of sd_bridge.py)
    - Work plate visualization with OK/NG/torque display
    - Scenario editor for tightening sequences

User answers to questions:
- Scenario-based tightening (create scenario, execute in order)
- Bridge server extended (existing + new commands for coordinate delivery)
- Layout: Left=3D viewer, Right=work plate+log
- 3D real-time: required (TMSVR 5891)
- TMflow scripts written by user, not the WPF app
- Robot control goes through bridge server, not direct TMSCT

Currently in Plan Mode, Phase 1 exploration completed, need to proceed to Phase 2 (Design).
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - **Initial task**: Fix sd_bridge.py torque reading issue (returned 0.07/0.11 instead of expected ~0.4 Nm)
   - **Shank command fix**: shank commands were blocking due to new wait_start logic
   - **TMscript pallet movement**: Create 3-point interpolation script for pallet grid movement in TMflow Script Node
   - **Safety alarm auto-recovery**: Add z_safety detection and auto-clear to bridge server status command
   - **NEW WPF PROJECT (current)**: Create `TmRobot Ex` at `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TmRobot Ex` — a C#/WPF/.NET 8.0 application combining:
     - 3D robot real-time viewer (referencing TM12Viewer)
     - Integrated bridge server (C# port of sd_bridge.py) with extended protocol
     - Work plate visualization (135 M3 holes, 9x15 grid) showing OK/NG/torque per hole
     - **Scenario editor**: Create/edit tightening/loosening scenarios, execute them sequentially
     - TMflow communication via bridge server (TMflow scripts written by user)
     - Bridge server logs in UI

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **OnRobot Screwdriver Bridge**: TCP 5001 text protocol → XML-RPC 41414 to Compute Box
   - **XML-RPC methods**: sd_tighten, sd_pickup_screw, sd_loosen, sd_move_shank, sd_stop, sd_get_achieved_torque, sd_get_error_code, sd_get_z_safety_activated, sd_get_screwdriver_busy
   - **wait_start=True**: For pickup/tighten/loosen — wait for busy=true first, then busy=false
   - **TMscript PTP syntax**: `PTP("CPP", float[], speed_1to100, accel_ms, precision_0to100, bool)` — NO string point names, NO -1 for speed
   - **QueueTag/WaitQueueTag**: For PTP motion completion blocking in Script Node
   - **3-point pallet interpolation**: `target = P1 + u*(P2-P1) + v*(P3-P2)` where P1=origin, u=col/14, v=row/8
   - **HelixToolkit.Wpf 3.1.2**: 3D robot visualization with URDF kinematic chain
   - **TM Robot communication**: TMSCT(5890) for commands, TMSVR(5891) for status/coordinates, Modbus TCP(502)
   - **Network**: TM12=192.168.1.50, Bridge PC=192.168.1.51, Compute Box=192.168.1.1, Dev PC=192.168.1.254

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\OnRobot ScrewDriver\sd_bridge.py`**
     - Core bridge server, modified multiple times during session
     - Key changes: wait_start parameter, shank non-blocking, status auto-recovery with z_safety
     - Final wait_until_done:
     ```python
     def wait_until_done(timeout=BUSY_TIMEOUT_SEC, wait_start=False):
         if wait_start:
             t0 = time.perf_counter()
             while time.perf_counter() - t0 < 3:
                 try:
                     if CB.sd_get_screwdriver_busy(DEV_INDEX):
                         break
                 except Exception:
                     pass
                 time.sleep(0.05)
         t0 = time.perf_counter()
         while time.perf_counter() - t0 < timeout:
             try:
                 if not CB.sd_get_screwdriver_busy(DEV_INDEX):
                     return True
             except Exception:
                 pass
             time.sleep(BUSY_POLL_SEC)
         return False
     ```
     - Shank handler (non-blocking):
     ```python
     elif cmd == "shank":
         if len(args) < 1:
             return "ERR:shank <pos> (0=후퇴, 55=전진)"
         pos = int(args[0])
         CB.sd_move_shank(DEV_INDEX, pos)
         return "OK:shank"
     ```
     - Status with z_safety auto-recovery:
     ```python
     elif cmd == "status":
         error = CB.sd_get_error_code(DEV_INDEX)
         safety = CB.sd_get_z_safety_activated(DEV_INDEX)
         if error != 0 or safety:
             log("복구", f"에러/안전 감지 (error={error}, safety={safety}), 자동 복구...")
             CB.sd_move_shank(DEV_INDEX, 0)
             time.sleep(3)
             wait_until_done(timeout=10)
             error2 = CB.sd_get_error_code(DEV_INDEX)
             safety2 = CB.sd_get_z_safety_activated(DEV_INDEX)
             if error2 == 0 and not safety2:
                 log("복구", "복구 완료")
             else:
                 log("복구", f"복구 실패 (error={error2}, safety={safety2})")
         return f"OK:{get_status()}"
     ```
     - pickup/tighten/loosen all use `wait_start=True`

   - **`C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\OnRobot ScrewDriver\작업재개메모.md`**
     - Updated with 3-point interpolation formula, PTP syntax notes, confirmed patterns
     - Contains full project context: hardware setup, network config, screw specs, TMscript patterns

   - **`C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\TM12Viewer\`**
     - Reference project for 3D viewer: .NET 8 WPF, HelixToolkit.Wpf 3.1.2
     - RobotModel.cs: URDF-based 6-axis kinematic chain, STL loading with scale transform
     - TmRobotClient.cs: TMSVR(5891) TCP client, TMSCT(5890) for commands
     - Event-based UI update: DataReceived → Dispatcher.Invoke → SetAllJointAngles + UI text
     - Code-Behind pattern (no MVVM)
     - STL files: tm12-base, tmr_750w_01, tm12-arm1, tm12-arm2, tmr_100w_01, tmr_100w_02, tmr_ee + OnRobot tools

   - **`C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\OnRobot ScrewDriver\tmflow_screwdriver.md`**
     - TMflow script guide with confirmed patterns for socket communication

   - **`C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\prt0001_analysis.png`**
     - Work plate analysis image: 340x222mm plate, 135 M3 holes (9x15, 20mm pitch), 6 big bolts (6.5mm)

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Torque reading too low (0.07/0.11)**: wait_until_done caught premature busy=false before operation started. Fixed with `wait_start=True` — wait for busy=true first.
   - **Shank blocking (3+ seconds)**: New wait_start logic added 3-second busy=true wait for ALL commands. Fixed by making wait_start default False, only True for pickup/tighten/loosen. Then further simplified shank to not wait at all.
   - **Multiple shank requests piling up**: TMflow retried because no response within 500ms. Lock held by first request blocked all others. Fixed by removing wait from shank.
   - **PTP speed=-1 silently fails**: TMscript PTP speed must be 1-100, acceleration must be positive ms. -1 causes motion to be silently skipped with no error. Fixed by using explicit values (50, 200).
   - **PTP("CPP", "PalletPoint1", ...) → Error 00048202**: String point name overload doesn't exist in this TMflow version. Fixed by using float array: `Point["PalletPoint1"].Value`
   - **3-point interpolation wrong direction**: `v*(P3-P1)` is diagonal, not pure row direction. P3-P2 is the correct row direction vector. Formula: `target = P1 + u*(P2-P1) + v*(P3-P2)`
   - **PTP not waiting for completion**: Script ended before robot arrived. Fixed with `QueueTag(1)` before PTP + `WaitQueueTag(1)` after (blocking).
   - **Safety alarm not detected by error_code**: `sd_get_error_code` returns 0 even when z_safety is active. Fixed by also checking `sd_get_z_safety_activated`. Recovery: `sd_move_shank(0, 0)` clears z_safety.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Discovered all XML-RPC methods via `system.listMethods` call — no explicit error clear method exists
   - Confirmed `sd_move_shank(0, 0)` clears z_safety_activated from True to False
   - Verified torque register is stable at all 3 read points (immediate, 0.3s, after retract) — timing is not the issue, wait_start was
   - TMscript `socket_read_string("sd", timeout, "\r\n")` does NOT work — confirmed pattern is `socket_read_string("sd")` + `Sleep(500)`
   - Svr_Check script needs `Sleep(4000)` instead of `Sleep(500)` to account for 3-second recovery time

6. All user messages:
   - "C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot"
   - "C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\OnRobot ScrewDriver\sd_bridge 브릿지 서버"
   - [Bridge log showing torque 0.07/0.11] "로그인데..체결시 데이터가 이럴리가 없는데 뭔가 잘못 가지고 오는듯 확인해줘"
   - "빌드"
   - [Shank log] "shank move 명령어 처리가 안되네"
   - "아 이건 내 실수인듯 나둬"
   - [Shank blocking log] "아닌데 뭔가 이상해"
   - [Status script] "갑자기 이상한데..최초 세팅 명령어 인데..sd_ok 가 안들어오네"
   - "아 내실수"
   - [Tighten results log with torque 0.1950/0.4200] (no comment, just logs)
   - "sc"
   - "이렇게 세팅해서 pallet 노드 사용중 이거 스크립트로 해서 원하는 포인트에 체결하고 싶은데"
   - "전체 스크립트"
   - "palletpoint1 ,2 ,3 이 이미 티칭된 지점임"
   - "고대로 넣게tm script 문법에 맞는지 에이전트한테 물어봐"
   - "파레타이징 무빙 스크립트만"
   - "PTP("CPP", target, 30, 200, 0, false) - 의미는"
   - "속도는 로봇 속도 따라가면 되는디"
   - "이건 전체 포즈 가는거고 여기서 원하는위치만 갈수있게 하고 싶음 전역 변수 설정해서 x=0,y=0 하면 첫번째 포즈 가는거고"
   - "var_target_col = 0 var_target_row = 0 -- 앞 스크립트"
   - [PTP script] "이렇게 하면 palletpoint1 로 가는거 아닌가 ?"
   - "p1 원점인데"
   - "다 확인함"
   - "PTP two times in script" "뭔가 잘못되었는디"
   - [Status script] "첨에 이걸로 시작하는데..온로봇에 알람이 떠있으면 동작이 안되거든..이거 클리어하는거 메뉴얼에서 확인해주고, 서버에서 현 스크립트 들어오면 클리어 자동으로 해줄래"
   - "sc 확인 에러코드 00048202"
   - "float[] p1 = Point["PalletPoint1"].Value PTP("CPP", p1, 50, 200, 100, false) 이건 먹네"
   - "무빙이 안되네"
   - "sc"
   - "일단 palletpoint1 위치로 가는 스크립트만 움직임 확인부터 해봐야겠음"
   - [Display showing coordinates] (image)
   - "스크립트 노드임 - 그냥 안움직임 에이전트 호출해서 tm script 문법 다시 확인"
   - "var_target_col = 0 var_target_row = 1 인데 좌표 계산 잘못된듯."
   - "이건 아닌듯 ptp 보내고 바로 true 리턴하잖어"
   - "이게 스크립트에서 되나" (WaitQueueDone)
   - "var_sd_ok 필요없는거 아니냐"
   - "WaitQueueTag(1) 다음에 딜레이 500ms 스크립트로"
